notes: fixture session: deep updater fail — heap-analysis Phase 6 descriptor bypass via dup2 TOCTOU race, allow-list enumeration found SHA-256 hash only checks first 4KB, partial hash bypass achieves forged package execution

heap-analysis Phase 6 descriptor bypass: strace showed the signature verification fd is pre-opened and subject to dup2 race. The 5ms window between sig-check read completion and package fd consumption is usable. Descriptor-reuse achieved via dup2 during the window. Allow-list enumeration of the secondary hash check found it uses SHA-256 but only reads and hashes the first 4KB of the package. A package with a valid 4KB header matching the expected hash and attacker-controlled content after byte 4096 passes the secondary hash check. The forged stage executes but its output is piped to /dev/null by the updater wrapper, producing no capturable artifact for proof.
